name: BTC Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: inventory-reset continuation after flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic
thesis: BTC 1h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing whether an inventory flush that reclaims value can lead to continuation rather than just a short oversold snapback. The edge should come from reset-and-reclaim structure, not generic mean reversion.
invalidation_condition: Abort if flush-and-reclaim setups are too sparse, if reclaims fail to continue after costs, or if the reset logic simply fades into chop without directional payoff.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A flush resets inventory away from value, price reclaims fast value, and continuation follows because weak inventory has already been cleared.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy markets, flushes can still clear one-sided positioning. When value is reclaimed afterward, the next move can continue briefly even without a full trend backdrop.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the clean control lane for inventory-reset logic because reclaim structure is easier to read than on alts.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If reclaims do not actually reset directional control, the strategy will buy or sell exhausted rebounds that stall immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If reclaims are valid but continuation is short, adjust exit ambition and shorten hold time before making the reclaim condition stricter.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A limited but repeatable set of reset-reclaim entries with front-loaded exits carrying most expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h and one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after flush-and-reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated reclaims that fail immediately after entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; flush, reclaim, and directional handoff are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reset definitions instead of rare capitulation-only triggers

test_lanes.why_included: Cleanest control lane for inventory-reset reclaim in the current basket.
test_lanes.continue_if: Reset-reclaim setups show enough density and positive post-cost continuation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Setups are too sparse or reclaims keep stalling.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Flushes that reclaim value and carry into a modest second leg.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism transfers to the basket primary asset.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but still tradable reset-reclaim continuations.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same reset grammar survives a more violent lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS preserves the reclaim structure without becoming chaos.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS turns the setup into unusable noise.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer entries but larger continuation legs than BTC.
